name: JetBrains Junie
description: AI coding agent embedded in IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, WebStorm and other JetBrains IDEs; explores the project, plans, writes and tests code.

first-release.comment: Announced/EAP late Jan 2025; reached general availability on 2025-04-16 (in IntelliJ IDEA Ultimate, PyCharm Pro, WebStorm, GoLand).

status.comment: GA since April 2025, now shipped across most JetBrains IDEs plus a CLI. Checked 2026-06-05.

license.comment: Agent is proprietary, governed by JetBrains AI Service Terms; the JetBrains/junie repo is publicly visible (source-available distribution) but not OSI-licensed. Treating as proprietary.

specialization.comment: Broad coding agent for bug fixes, feature implementation, refactoring and tests.

interface.comment: Primary form is an IDE plugin for JetBrains IDEs; JetBrains also ships a Junie CLI (terminal) and a GitHub Action for CI/CD.

host-ides.comment: Runs in JetBrains IDEs (IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, WebStorm, GoLand, PhpStorm, RubyMine, RustRover, Rider, CLion, Android Studio) and in the terminal via the Junie CLI. No VS Code plugin (only a feature request as of check).

open-source.comment: Source is publicly visible on GitHub but governed by proprietary JetBrains AI Service Terms, not an OSI license.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Ships as a JetBrains IDE plugin (extension) and as a standalone CLI tool; also a GitHub Action for CI/CD.

default-model-provider.comment: Default is a dynamically selected best price-quality model; users can pick across Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI and OpenRouter. No single fixed provider.

model-choice.comment: Models selectable across providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI, OpenRouter) via JetBrains AI subscription or CLI flags.

bring-your-own-key.comment: BYOK supported for OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI and OpenRouter keys.

local-model-support.comment: JetBrains AI/Junie governance includes local/on-premises and offline model options (Enterprise); not as turnkey as Ollama presets, so noted with moderate confidence.

multi-file-editing.comment: Can introduce large-scale edits across the project as part of a single task.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Code mode breaks a task into a multistep plan, then writes code, runs tests/terminal commands and iterates.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs terminal commands and tests; gated by an action allowlist / approval unless Brave Mode is enabled.

mcp-support.comment: Connects to MCP servers for external tools/data sources.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Supports custom subagents stored in .junie/agents/; the main agent discovers and delegates focused subtasks to them.

codebase-indexing.comment: Leverages the JetBrains IDE's native project index / code intelligence for retrieval rather than only ad-hoc file reads. In the IDE form factor this is the IDE's built index.

planning-mode.comment: Builds an explicit multistep plan before executing; Ask mode offers read-only analysis/planning.

multimodal-input.comment: Image/screenshot input is an open feature request in JetBrains YouTrack, not yet shipped as of check (2026-06-05).

browser-computer-use.comment: No documented capability to drive a real browser/GUI to verify its own changes; MCP can add such tools but it is not built in.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Model-driven delegation: the main agent autonomously discovers all available subagents and selects/runs the relevant one by matching its name and description to the task. Docs state custom subagents cannot be invoked manually via slash commands; they are only called automatically through delegation. Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Ask mode (interactive), Code mode with approvals (supervised), Brave Mode (autonomous), and the GitHub Action runs async in CI/CD. No tab inline completion (that is AI Assistant's job).

inline-completion.comment: Junie is the agent; as-you-type tab completion is handled by the separate JetBrains AI Assistant, not Junie.

background-async-execution.comment: Junie GitHub Action runs tasks asynchronously in CI/CD (issue-to-PR, fix CI failures) detached from the developer's session.

persistent-memory.comment: Persistent reusable context via guideline files (.junie/AGENTS.md / AGENTS.md) at project and user-global (~/.junie) scope, added to every task across sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Can undo specific file changes or revert the whole project to earlier checkpoints in the conversation.

git-integration.comment: Native git awareness; runs inside JetBrains IDE VCS and the GitHub Action makes branches/commits.

pr-creation.comment: Junie GitHub Action creates pull requests from issues/comments and posts inline review comments.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Project-level guideline files: .junie/AGENTS.md, AGENTS.md, or legacy .junie/guidelines.md.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via custom slash commands, agent skills, custom subagents, MCP servers, and reusable extension bundles.

permission-model.comment: Default asks approval per sensitive action (action allowlist); Brave Mode can auto-run all actions, so posture is configurable.

self-host-on-prem.comment: AI Enterprise unlocks local/on-premises model options and offline/local-only modes.

privacy-mode.comment: JetBrains states code is never used for AI training and data is not persisted unless the user opts into Detailed Data Collection.

enterprise-controls.comment: JetBrains AI Enterprise provides SSO, audit logs, profile-based policies, provider selection and on-prem/local options.

pricing-model.comment: AI Free tier with a small monthly credit quota; paid AI Pro/Ultimate subscriptions; credits can be topped up usage-based ($1/credit). BYOK also possible.

free-tier.comment: AI Free plan ($0) includes unlimited local AI plus a small cloud credit quota (~3 credits/30 days).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: AI Pro is $10/mo (10 credits); AI Ultimate $30/mo. Junie is included with the JetBrains AI subscription. Checked 2026-06-05.

maturity.comment: GA for over a year, backed by JetBrains and shipped across most of its IDEs plus a CLI; stable and widely deployed but still rapidly adding capabilities.

community-size.comment: Reaches the large JetBrains IDE user base; widely distributed via the Marketplace, though smaller than Copilot/Cursor scale.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Marketplace-based extensions and the open Agent Skills format, plus MCP server ecosystem.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md (and .junie/AGENTS.md); legacy .junie/guidelines.md is the vendor-specific (custom) filename.

memory-scopes.comment: Project guidelines (repo) plus user-global guidelines at ~/.junie/AGENTS.md, with project taking precedence on conflicts.

scoped-rules-files.comment: Guidelines are always-applied; no documented path-based/file-type conditional rule activation for guideline files.

auto-memory.comment: Persistent context relies on user-authored guideline files; no documented autonomous cross-session memory the agent writes itself.

skill-support.comment: Supports Agent Skills (folders with a SKILL.md) loaded on demand, plus custom slash commands and subagents.

skill-invocation.comment: Agent skills are selected and loaded by the model when relevant (model-callable); custom slash commands are explicitly triggered by the user (user-callable).

always-on-surface.comment: At startup only skill name+description are loaded; the full SKILL.md is pulled into context only when the relevant skill is invoked (progressive disclosure).

skill-distribution.comment: Skills/extensions install via git-based marketplaces, git clone, or by placing the skill folder under .junie/skills manually.

sandboxing-technology.comment: Junie executes commands locally on the developer's machine with the user's own privileges; isolation is by approval/allowlist, not a kernel sandbox, container or VM. The GitHub Action runs on standard CI runners (host infra, not Junie's own isolation), and JetBrains is testing a cloud sandbox but it is not the default. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: allowlist.json gates four action categories (executables, mcpTools, fileEditing, readOutsideProject) = per-tool; per-command approval with prefix/glob pattern rules = per-command + command-prefix; separate readOutsideProject vs fileEditing and an inside/outside-project distinction = read-write-distinction + per-path. No per-network-domain gating documented.

rule-precedence.comment: allowlist.json rules are evaluated top to bottom, first match taking precedence (e.g. an 'ask' rule for 'npm install *' placed above an 'allow' prefix rule for 'npm' wins) — user-controlled ordered evaluation.

permission-configurability.comment: User authors allow/ask rules (prefix or glob pattern) in ~/.junie/allowlist.json. No programmable hook/policy-script decision point for permission evaluation documented.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No default deny patterns for secrets (.env, key files) ship enabled; the docs describe only what users can allow. Users can restrict access via a user-authored .aiignore file, but that is opt-in, so default protection is false. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: The 'Always allow' / 'Allow this command' / 'Allow similar commands' approval options write the command to the persistent ~/.junie/allowlist.json so it runs without approval in future Junie runs (survives restarts).

unsupervised-mode.comment: Brave Mode (/brave slash command or Ctrl+B) authorizes Junie to execute all potentially sensitive actions without approval for hands-off runs (documented but not recommended).

network-access-control.comment: Junie runs locally with full machine network access; no documented outbound-egress gating, domain allowlist or proxy for the agent's own runs (network-touching commands are gated only as terminal commands by the action allowlist, not as a network policy). Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out of the box Junie edits files within the project without prompting but asks for approval before running terminal commands, MCP tools, tests/builds, and reading/editing files outside the project (only ls, cd, pwd run without approval).
